sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the bottom bar: purple (22 pixels, cols 0-21) and white (42 pixels, cols 22-63). The purple bar seems to represent progress (22/64 = ~34%). Since all green blocks are collected, maybe the goal isn't just collecting them. Perhaps the player needs to navigate to a specific target location.
Looking at the image, I see the blue region has a specific shape. There might be a target area the player needs to reach. Let me look for any special markers or try moving the player to different parts of the blue region to see what happens. Let me also check if there's a dark gray (G) object that might be a target.
The G objects are dark gray - 144 of them. These could be walls or decorations. Let me check if the player needs to reach a specific area. Maybe I should try navigating the player to the top of the blue region or to a corner.
Let me try moving the player around to see if reaching certain areas triggers level completion.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# All green blocks collected but level not done. 
# Maybe player needs to navigate to a specific target.
# Let me look at the blue region boundary to find potential targets
seg = current_frame.segmentation
blue = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S'][0]
print(f"Blue boundary ({len(blue['boundary'])} points):")
for p in blue['boundary']:
    print(f"  {p}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait - let me re-read the valid actions: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7. There's no UP/DOWN. So movement might only be horizontal. But MOUSE seems to scroll the view rather than move the player.
